2026 National Security Hackathon | Registration Now Open

By Maggie Gray

I am excited to announce that registration is now open for the third annual National Security Hackathon, taking place May 1-3 in San Francisco! This year, we are proud to be formally partnering with the US Army FUZE team, Stanford DEFCON Technology and National Security Student Network, and UC Berkeley's new Defense and Dual Use Technology Club to bring together the brightest minds across defense, academia, and industry.

$50,000 in cash prizes is on the table across four critical challenge tracks. Something special about this hackathon: because we are using Army prize challenge authority to award prizes, hackathon winners will be able to win follow on prototyping OT contracts from DoW following the event.

Shield Capital is proud to co-host this event alongside Cerebral Valley, Palantir, IQT, DCVC, OpenAI, Scale AI, Armada, Deloitte, TENEX, Shack15, and Skyrun. If you're a builder, coder, designer, student, engineer, or analyst looking to apply your skills to problems that actually matter—this is your moment.

Hope to see you there.
